name: ezoff

metadata.name: ezoff
metadata.summary: Python package that acts as a wrapper for the EZOffice API.

documentation:
# ezoff

Python package for interacting with the EZOffice API

## Installation

`pip install ezoff`

## Usage

Several environment variables are required for ezo to function.

| Required? | Env Variable | Description |
| --------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| EZO_BASE_URL | Yes | Should be https://{companyname}.ezofficeinventory.com/ |
| EZO_TOKEN | Yes | The access token used to authenticate requests |

## Project Structure

Project is split up into several files depending on what area of the EZOffice API is being dealt with. Purely for organizational purposes.

### Assets

Contains functions for the following:

- get all asssets
- get filtered assets
- search for an asset
- create an asset
- update an asset
- delete an asset
- check asset in
- check asset out
- get an asset's history

### Inventories

- get inventories
- get inventory details
- create inventory order
- get inventory history

### Groups

Contains functions for the following:

- get subgroups

### Locations

Contains functions for the following:

- get locations
- get location details
- get item quantities in location
- create a location
- activate a location
- deactivate a location
- update a location

### Members

Contains functions for the following:

- get members
- get a member's details
- create a member
- update a member
- deactivate a member
- activate a member
- get custom roles
- get teams

### Work Orders

Contains functions for the following:

- get work orders
- get work order details
- get work order types
- create a work order
- start a work order
- end a work order
- add work log to a work order
- add linked inventory to a work order
- get checklists

## Notes

The official EZOffice documentation is mistaken on custom fields (insofar as how to fill them out when creating an object or updating the custom field on an already existing object). It says to put underscores in place of spaces in the field name, but this is incorrect. After testing the API, it appears it wants the actual name of the field with the spaces, not underscores. At least on members.

Similarly, the documentation isn't exhaustive when it comes to listing the valid fields when creating or updating something. Frequently there are fields on the actual page that aren't mentioned in the EZOffice documentation. Throughout this program I check that keys provided are valid to prevent the API call from potentially erroring out. So there may be times where a key that would be valid if passed to the API is provided, but the function does not allow it. This is because the key wasn't documented. Just have to add to list of valid key whenever we run into one.

When wanting to clear a field out of its current value with an update function, generally the empty string ("") should be used.

modules.name: ezoff._helpers
documentation: Helper functions for ezoff

functions.name: ezoff._helpers._fetch_page
documentation: Wrapper around requests.get that retries on RequestException

modules.name: ezoff._auth
documentation:
Handles any authentication-realted functionality.
Largely just checking if the required environment variables are set.

methods.name: ezoff._auth.Decorators.check_env_vars
documentation: Decorator to check if the required environment variables are set

modules.name: ezoff.ezoff
documentation: Module for interacting with the EZOffice Inventory API

modules.name: ezoff.inventory
documentation: Covers everything related to inventory assets.

functions.name: ezoff.inventory.get_all_inventories
documentation:
Gets all inventory assets. Recommended to use the filtered endpoint instead
as this one will take a long time to run.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrieve-inventories

functions.name: ezoff.inventory.get_inventory_details
documentation:
Get details for an inventory asset (sometimes referred to in API as a volatile asset)
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-volatile-details
@Decorators.check_env_vars
def get_filtered_inventories(filter: dict):
Gets inventory assets that match the given filters
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-volatile-asset-filters
url = os.environ["EZO_BASE_URL"] + "inventory/filter.api"

functions.name: ezoff.inventory.create_inventory_order
documentation: Creates an inventory order (i.e. Add Stock or New Sale)

functions.name: ezoff.inventory.get_inventory_history
documentation:
Gets history of an inventory asset
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-inventory-history
@Decorators.check_env_vars
def transfer_inventory(inv_asset_num: int, transfer: dict) -> dict:
Transfers inventory asset amount from one location to another.
Called "transfer stock" in the documentation, I'm just calling it
transfer inventory for consistency.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-transfer-stock
url = os.environ["EZO_BASE_URL"] + f"inventory/{inv_asset_num}/transfer_stock.api"

modules.name: ezoff.groups
documentation: Covers everything related to groups and subgroups in EZOfficeInventory

functions.name: ezoff.groups.get_subgroups
documentation:
Get subgroups
Optionally takes a group_id to get subgroups of a specific group

modules.name: ezoff.members
documentation: This module contains functions for interacting with members/roles/user setup in EZOfficeInventory

functions.name: ezoff.members.get_members
documentation:
Get members from EZOfficeInventory
Optionally filter by email, employee_identification_number, or status
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrieve-members

functions.name: ezoff.members.get_member_details
documentation:
Get member from EZOfficeInventory by member_id
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-member-details

functions.name: ezoff.members.create_member
documentation:
Create a new member
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-create-member

functions.name: ezoff.members.update_member
documentation:
Update a member
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-update-member

functions.name: ezoff.members.deactivate_member
documentation:
Deactivate a member
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-deactivate-user

functions.name: ezoff.members.activate_member
documentation:
Activate a member
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-activate-user

functions.name: ezoff.members.get_custom_roles
documentation:
Get list of custom roles
Results are technically paginated but the number of custom roles
is usually small enough that it can be returned in one page.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrieve-roles

functions.name: ezoff.members.get_teams
documentation:
Get teams
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrieve-teams

modules.name: ezoff.workorders
documentation: This module contains functions to interact with work orders in EZOfficeInventory.

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.get_work_orders
documentation:
Get filtered work orders.

Note: This endpoint is weird. It supports many more filters than the
documentation advertises. There is a corresponding filter for each
of the filter options in the EZOffice web interface.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-get-filtered-task

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.get_work_order_details
documentation:
Get work order details
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrive-task-details

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.get_work_order_types
documentation:
Get work order types
Function doesn't appear to be paginated even though most other similar
functions are.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-get-task-types

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.create_work_order
documentation:
Create a work order
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-create-task

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.start_work_order
documentation:
Start a work order
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-start-task

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.end_work_order
documentation:
End a work order
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-end-task

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.add_work_log_to_work_order
documentation:
Add a work log to a work order
resource id and resource type vary depending on type of component
work log is being added against. Asset vs Group vs Member etc. Docu has a table
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-add-work-log-to-task

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.add_linked_inv_to_work_order
documentation:
Add linked inventory items to a work order
resource id and resource type vary depending on type of component
linked inventory is being added against. Asset vs Group vs Member etc. Docu has a table
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-add-linked-inventory-to-task

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.get_checklists
documentation:
Get checklists
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrieve-checklists

functions.name: ezoff.workorders.create_service
documentation:
Creates a service record against a given asset
<https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-create-service>

```
param asset_id

The ID of the asset to create the service record against

param service

A dictionary containing the service record details
```

modules.name: ezoff.assets
documentation: Covers everything related to fixed assets in EZOffice

functions.name: ezoff.assets.get_asset_details
documentation:
Gets asset details
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-asset-details

functions.name: ezoff.assets.get_all_assets
documentation:
Get assets
Recommended to use endpoint that takes a filter instead.
This endpoint can be slow as it returns all assets in the system. Potentially
several hundred pages of assets.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retrive-assets

functions.name: ezoff.assets.get_filtered_assets
documentation:
Get assets via filtering. Recommended to use this endpoint rather than
returning all assets.

functions.name: ezoff.assets.search_for_asset
documentation:
Search for an asset.
The equivalent of the search bar in the EZOfficeInventory UI.
May not return all assets that match the search term. Better to use
get_filtered_assets if you want to return all assets that match a filter.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-search-name

functions.name: ezoff.assets.create_asset
documentation:
Create an asset
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-create-asset

functions.name: ezoff.assets.update_asset
documentation:
Update an asset's details
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-update-asset

functions.name: ezoff.assets.delete_asset
documentation:
Delete an asset
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-delete-asset

functions.name: ezoff.assets.checkin_asset
documentation:
Check in an asset to a location
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-checkin-asset

functions.name: ezoff.assets.checkout_asset
documentation:
Check out an asset to a member
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-checkout-asset

Note: If user is inactive, checkout will return a 200 status code but the
asset will not be checked out. Response will contain a message.

functions.name: ezoff.assets.retire_asset
documentation:
Retires an asset. Asset needs to be in an available state to retire.
<https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retire-asset>

```
param asset_id

The asset ID to retire

param retire

A dictionary containing the retirement details. Must contain the keys fixed_asset[retire_reason_id] and fixed_asset[retired_on]
```

functions.name: ezoff.assets.reactivate_asset
documentation:
Reactivates a retired asset.
<https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-activate-asset>

```
param asset_id

The asset ID to reactivate

param reactivate

A dictionary containing the reactivation details. Currently that's only the key fixed_asset[location_id]. Whether it's required or not varies depending on company settings.
```

functions.name: ezoff.assets.get_asset_history
documentation:
Get asset history
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-checkin-out-history

functions.name: ezoff.assets.get_items_for_token_input
documentation:
This isn't an official endpoint in the EZOfficeInventory API. It's used to populate
the token input dropdowns in the EZOfficeInventory UI. However, still works if called
and is needed if wanting to use the get_work_orders item filter. Which doesn't yet
support the asset ID as a filter. But does support the ID that comes from this endpoint.
Found this via the network tab in the browser. Not sure what the official name is
so I'm just going off of what the URL is.

Note: If you use "#{Asset Sequence Num}" as the q search parameter, it should
only return one result. If you use a more general search term. like searching
for the name, you may get multiple.

modules.name: ezoff.locations
documentation: This module contains functions for interacting with locations in EZOfficeInventory

functions.name: ezoff.locations.get_locations
documentation:
Get locations
Optionally filter by status
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-retreive-locations

functions.name: ezoff.locations.get_location_details
documentation:
Get location details
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-location-details

functions.name: ezoff.locations.get_location_item_quantities
documentation: Get quantities of each item at a location

functions.name: ezoff.locations.create_location
documentation:
Create a location
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-create-location

functions.name: ezoff.locations.activate_location
documentation:
Activate a location
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-activate-location

functions.name: ezoff.locations.deactivate_location
documentation:
Deactivate a location
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-deactivate-location

functions.name: ezoff.locations.update_location
documentation:
Updates a location.
Note: The location's parent ID (location[parent_id]) is a required field for this call,
because EZOffice will wipe out whatever is currently in that field
if you don't include it. No idea why. If you're not changing the parent
location of the location, you just use the current parent ID. The documentation
also suggests that location[name] is a required field, but that doesn't appear
to be the case. Not sure if documentation is wrong or I'm misunderstanding what
they're saying.
https://ezo.io/ezofficeinventory/developers/#api-update-location
